notes: v1.7.3 — THE AS-BUILT LOOP: on-device, machine-readable as-built CSV export. PHANTOM consumes the Master but never produced truth; the real US-SPK03 Master carried ZERO serials across 4,143 hosts. This closes the loop — plan in, build, verified truth out — emitting the metal PHANTOM already captures as a byproduct (Metal Match scans, Audit Walk verdicts, slot serials, audit trail). ENGINE: deploy_buildAsBuilt(deployId) joins, per planned position: THE PLAN (deploy_loadRacks slots -> cab_id=rack.rackId, ru=slot.uStart, expected_model=slot.model, expected_dns=slot.dns [Master only], planned_serial=slot.serial [Master only, often blank]); BUILD STATE (deploy_computeRackRollup -> rack_status/phase_pct; completed_by from the latest PHASE_COMPLETE audit actor with phase.signedOffBy fallback; completed_at from max phase.signedOffAt as ISO); VERIFICATION (Audit Walk verdict per position from phantom_audit_walk_v1 keyed String(uStart), trusted ONLY when slotSig matches the slot count; scanned_serial bound to a position by exact planned_serial match against the phantom_scan_collection asset scans, using each scan's FROZEN mm verdict). VERDICT (derived, honesty-locked): VERIFIED (bound scan, frozen Metal Match found/dup), MISMATCH (bound scan, mm unknown), UNVERIFIED (built or audit-walked but no confirming serial scan), MISSING (planned, zero evidence), EXTRA (one row per captured asset scan that matches no planned position — where the metal the Master lacks actually lands). EMPIRICAL REALITY surfaced by the build: because the Master has zero serials, _metalMatch returns 'no-serials' and planned_serial is blank, so on real data NO plan row can be VERIFIED-by-scan — the export honestly shows MISSING/UNVERIFIED plan rows + EXTRA captured-serial rows + Audit Walk pass/fail. That IS the loop's value. INVARIANT HONESTY: no value is ever synthesized/defaulted/inferred into a serial or verdict; blank stays blank; UNVERIFIED means unverified. Single-source column const ASBUILT_COLUMNS (re-keyable in one line after John's NetBox-ingest conversation): site_code, deployment, exported_at, cab_id, ru, expected_model, expected_dns, planned_serial, scanned_serial, verdict, audit_walk, rack_status, phase_pct, completed_by, completed_at, notes. DELIVERY: CSV (RFC-4180 escape mirroring deploy_buildCSV's csvEsc) via the existing downloadCSV + navigator.share(files) path (BOM, iOS share sheet, download fallback); 100% on-device, no network/Worker, derived-not-stored (re-export recomputes). ENTRY POINTS (two): EXPORT AS-BUILT button in deployment detail (always available — Master-scoped and legacy), and a distinct EXPORT AS-BUILT button in the close-out modal (the hand-off moment), independent of the json/csv/both archive + purge gating. SCOPE: CSV only this ship (xlsx is a fast-follow; SheetJS write path is not yet wired); OUT = any NetBox/API/upload/background-sync (the pipe is a human handing a file over) and any edit/enrich at export time. Built in Claude Code on 756eff0 (v1.7.2) via recon-workflow (4 deep probes mapping plan/build/verify/delivery) -> inline build -> adversarial-review-workflow. node --check 2/2 + sw.js OK, JSON valid, block-0 braces UNCHANGED at 2566 (JS-only ship), three stamps v1.7.3. web-Claude parity review pending (sync brief covers through v1.7.2). VERIFY on-device: a fully-built+scanned deployment shows EXTRA rows with serials + UNVERIFIED/MISSING plan rows (no verdict inflation); legacy EDP export works with sparser columns; empty deployment yields header-only CSV; the iOS share sheet delivers the file. Minor deviation: filename follows downloadCSV's asbuilt-<site>-<deployment>-YYYY-MM-DD.csv convention (date, no HHMM) per the downloadCSV-reuse invariant.
